# Team Health Check Template: The Kasvu Garden Audit

Use this template alongside `frameworks/04-team-health-check-kasvu.md`.

---

## Pre-session (leader fills in alone)

**Date:**

**Team:**

**What triggered this health check?**

[ ] Scheduled quarterly audit
[ ] Significant change or transition
[ ] Sensing drift or disengagement
[ ] New leadership role
[ ] Something specific happened: ___
[ ] Other: ___

**What is your honest read on each pillar before the session?**

Rate each from 1 to 5. 1 = significantly broken. 5 = genuinely strong.

| Pillar | Your rating | What you are seeing |
|---|---|---|
| Culture and Engagement | | |
| Development | | |
| Process Improvement | | |
| Experimentation | | |
| Quality | | |
| Prospective | | |

**Which pillar are you most concerned about?**

**Which pillar do you think the team will rate lowest?**

**Which pillar do you think the team will be most reluctant to be honest about?**

**What would make this session feel safe enough for people to tell the truth?**

**Are you prepared to act on what emerges?**

[ ] Yes
[ ] I need to think about what acting on it would actually mean: ___

*(If no, reconsider running this session until you are ready. A health check that produces no change is worse than no health check.)*

---

## During session (shared with the team)

### Individual reflection scores

Ask each person to rate each pillar privately before sharing.

| Name | Culture & Engagement | Development | Process Improvement | Experimentation | Quality | Prospective |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| | | | | | | |
| | | | | | | |
| | | | | | | |
| | | | | | | |
| | | | | | | |
| | | | | | | |
| | | | | | | |
| | | | | | | |

**Range per pillar after sharing:**

| Pillar | Lowest score | Highest score | Spread |
|---|---|---|---|
| Culture and Engagement | | | |
| Development | | | |
| Process Improvement | | | |
| Experimentation | | | |
| Quality | | | |
| Prospective | | | |

*(A wide spread is more interesting than a low average. It means people are having different experiences of the same system.)*

---

### Share and surface

**Culture and Engagement — what the team said:**

**Development — what the team said:**

**Process Improvement — what the team said:**

**Experimentation — what the team said:**

**Quality — what the team said:**

**Prospective — what the team said:**

---

### Find the pattern

**What pattern does the team see across the six pillars?**

**Is there one pillar whose weakness is creating problems in the others?**

**If we could only improve one thing in the next 90 days, what would have the most impact?**

*(Let the team answer this. Do not answer it for them.)*

---

### One thing to tend

**What the team agreed to cultivate:**

**What it looks like right now:**

**What it would look like if genuinely healthy:**

**One specific change we will make:**

**Who is responsible for keeping it visible:**

**How we will know it is growing:**

**What would signal it is being neglected:**

---

### Close with appreciation

One thing each person values about this team.

| Name | What they value |
|---|---|
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |
| | |

---

## Post-session (leader fills in alone)

**What was the most honest moment in this session?**

**What did the ratings reveal that you did not already know?**

**What did the team avoid saying that still needs to be named?**

**Where was the spread widest and what does that tell you?**

**How safe did the room feel? What evidence are you basing that on?**

**What will you do differently as a leader based on what you heard?**

**What does this team need from you in the next 30 days?**

**On a scale of 1 to 5, how honest do you think the team was in this session?**

[ ] 1 — Surface level, people held back
[ ] 2 — Some honesty but significant gaps
[ ] 3 — Reasonably honest with some softening
[ ] 4 — Mostly honest, a few things unsaid
[ ] 5 — Genuinely honest, people said the hard things

**If below 3, what needs to change before the next audit for people to feel safer?**

**Date of next Garden Audit:**

---

Use this section to keep a running record across audits.
